ops.title: Trust security review (sol xhigh, 2026-07-18): LOCAL_PEERTOKEN unsound + 6 bounded blockers
ops.body:
Independent security review of the peer-trust layer at 5e3c9ce4 (read-only). CF/purego bridge, DR pinning (Developer ID anchor + quoted Team ID + identifier), and fail-closed paths all cleared. Two verdicts: NOT ship-ready.

**ARCHITECTURAL — LOCAL_PEERTOKEN is unsound as the code-auth binding (overturns the T1 "accept + document" decision):**
- **TF1 (High): fork/exec identity substitution.** XNU LOCAL_PEERTOKEN = proc_find(last_pid)+TASK_AUDIT_TOKEN. A same-UID attacker connects, forks a holder, execs the genuine hardened binary; the server reads the genuine identity and the holder sends attacker-controlled requests under it. No forgery/injection. Moving the query after the frame read only narrows it (send-then-exec persists).
- **TF2 (High): PID reuse binds the token to an unrelated process** (last_upid not validated), doesn't fail closed.
- **TF5 (Medium): the "same-UID floor confines delegation" rationale is false** across seteuid/setuid-exec/SCM_RIGHTS fd transfer (LOCAL_PEERCRED is connect-frozen; token is query-time).
- Disposition: LOCAL_PEERTOKEN cannot be the fleet code-authentication primitive. Per-message XPC/Mach audit-token authentication is REQUIRED for the security boundary — escalates task db24393 from "later/optional" to "required for any code-identity surface." → remaining-work plan.

**BOUNDED RELEASE-BLOCKER BUGS (fixing now, fable/security lane):**
- **TF3 (High): UID floor compares peer EFFECTIVE UID (xucred.cr_uid) with server REAL UID (os.Getuid).** A setuid-root daemon admits the user as same-UID. Fix: os.Geteuid() in Policy.Check + the server floor. (policy.go:96, wire/server.go:406)
- **TF4 (High): `daemonkit_unsigned` build tag is production-selectable and returns nil for a configured Requirement** → silent UID-only, violates the unconditional-ErrNoVerifier contract. Fix: remove the production-selectable bypass; test-only verifier seam. (trust/verify_unsigned.go)
- **TF6 (Medium): Server.Trust callback REPLACES the UID floor** (runs without a server-owned UID check). Fix: enforce effective-UID equality unconditionally, then the optional stricter callback. (wire/server.go:403)
- **TF7 (Medium): injection list omits com.apple.security.cs.allow-jit** (W+X MAP_JIT). Fix: reject when enabled. (verify_darwin.go:30)
- **TF8 (Medium): missing dlsym symbols PANIC** (purego.RegisterLibFunc) instead of ErrNoVerifier on framework skew. Fix: Dlsym with error propagation, publish the table atomically. (verify_darwin.go:68,149)
- **TF9 (Low): disable-library-validation ignored when CS_REQUIRE_LV/CS_FORCED_LV present** — deviates from strict contract. Fix: reject regardless. (verify_darwin.go:278)

**TESTS — TF10 (Low): wire.Peer is a 3-query non-atomic tuple; the E2E PID-match assertion overstates what it proves.** Add fork/exec, exit/reuse, SCM_RIGHTS, setuid, unsupported-option tests. → bundled with the bounded fixes / plan.

Wire concurrency review (same workflow): CONFIRMED ship-safe — formal capacity-invariant proof + 25x -race; its notes (handler panic process-fatal, cooperative shutdown, one test-strength gap) are by-design per crash-on-unexpected.
